title: carpinus polyneura
intro: Carpinus polyneura, also known as the Japanese hornbeam, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive horn-shaped buds and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Carpinus polyneura include the potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a tonic to promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the tree's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
